text-opt-bm

Benchmarking LLMs on text optimization

text-opt-bm gives an LLM agent a weak Python program and a scoring function. The agent repeatedly edits the program, and the benchmark records the best valid score it finds.

  • scoresMetrics include logical memory, compressed bytes, utility regret, and error rate.
  • perfect informationTwo tasks are scored on the same workload the agent optimizes.
  • generalizationFive tasks optimize on non-final feedback and are judged on a sealed test: tagging, compression, LLM routing, optimizer transfer, and SLM compression.
  • plotsCurves step at accepted submissions. Select any task card for protocol details.
Experiment 1

The current seven-task benchmark

  • tasksSeven retained tasks: two perfect-information systems problems and five generalization problems.
  • modelsFive independent one-hour trials per complete model/task series. A line is omitted unless all five trials are complete.
  • feedbackPerfect-information tasks optimize their final metric directly. Generalization tasks optimize reusable feedback and are evaluated on a sealed test.
  • figureThe overview normalizes each task to 1 at the starter and 0 at the best score found across complete plotted series. The memory-task cards use a post-first scale so the weak starter does not hide later optimization.
  • statusgpt-5.6-sol high and gpt-5.5 high are complete on all seven tasks. gpt-5.5 low is complete on the six non-SLM tasks; its affected SLM compression series is excluded.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes; evaluator-queue waits and campaign pauses are not charged.
All seven current tasks · complete N=5 model series only
gpt-5.6-sol highgpt-5.5 high
Completeness: gpt-5.6-sol high: 7/7 complete N=5 task series · gpt-5.5 high: 7/7 complete N=5 task series · gpt-5.5 low: 6/7 complete N=5 task series. The all-task mean is shown only when one model has all seven complete task series. Bands are ±1 standard deviation across the five trial-index aggregates.
Every current task · raw scores
Complete series only: every plotted model/task line contains five trials. The SLM sealed trajectories come from the post-run evaluation of every valid submission; optimizer-active time remains unchanged.
Experiment 2 · historical

Historical reasoning-effort and model sweep

  • tasksThe two retained perfect-information task families under their historical protocols.
  • settingsgpt-5.5 with high, low, and no reasoning effort; grok-4.5 with xhigh reasoning through Cursor.
  • runsFive one-hour runs per task and setting.
  • scopeSeparate gpt-5.5 xhigh saturation probes are excluded from these plots.
  • figureScores are normalized per task: 1 is the seed program, 0 is the best score found within the plotted runs. Bands show one standard deviation of the benchmark aggregate over independent per-task run choices.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Two perfect-information tasks, averaged by model and reasoning setting
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
Reading the bands: shaded regions are ±1 standard deviation of the benchmark aggregate over independent per-task run choices.
Every perfect-information task, 20 runs each (5 per setting), raw scores
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
individual runsetting mean
Task details: select any task card to reveal its objective and scoring method.
Experiment 3 · historical

Historical training feedback and sealed test

  • tasksThe two retained original generalization tasks.
  • gradingDuring optimization, the visible training set supplies the score.
  • testThe same selected programs are evaluated afterward on a sealed test.
  • settingsgpt-5.5 high, low, and none; grok-4.5 xhigh through Cursor.
  • scopecompress_heldout curves use the repaired plain-buffer scorer and a complete offline rescore of every featured submission.
  • figureLeft panel: train error. Right panel: sealed test error. Aggregate curves are normalized per task and averaged across the two tasks.
  • runsFive independent one-hour runs per task and model setting.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Two retained original generalization tasks, train set versus sealed test
Training · graded
Sealed test
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
Per task, train (graded) and sealed test, raw scores
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
individual runsetting mean
Experiment 4 · historical

Historical visible training feedback versus hidden validation

  • tasksThe same two retained generalization tasks as the training-feedback comparison.
  • visibleThe graded set is the full visible training set.
  • hiddenThe agent sees five examples and receives only an aggregate validation score on unseen data.
  • figureBoth conditions use the same sealed test. The three panels show train score, validation score, and sealed test score.
  • runsFive independent one-hour runs per task and feedback condition.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Two retained original generalization tasks, train, validation, and sealed test
Training
Hidden validation · graded
Sealed test
visible train gradinghidden validation grading
Per task, train, hidden validation (graded for the hidden arm), and sealed test, raw scores
visible train gradinghidden validation grading
individual runcondition mean
Experiment 5 · historical

Historical training-set size sweep

  • tasksThe two retained original generalization tasks with fixed distributions and fixed sealed tests.
  • sizesVisible train-to-test ratios are 1:4, 1:8, and 1:16.
  • figureLeft panel: train error. Right panel: sealed test error for the submitted programs.
  • runsFive independent one-hour runs per task and training-set size.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Two retained original generalization tasks, train-set size sweep
Training · graded
Sealed test
1:4 (largest train)1:81:16 (smallest)
Per task, train (graded) and sealed test, one line per training-set size, raw scores
1:4 (largest train)1:81:16 (smallest)
individual runsize mean

Fixed-method SLM reference study

  • studyRTN, HQQ, and AQLM were freshly evaluated on the current behavioral LFM2.5 protocol-v6 splits.
  • scopeThese are aggregate method results, not one-hour agent trajectories; the completed agent campaign is reported in Experiment 1.
  • metricMacro-average BF16 behavioral regression across GPQA, IFBench, single-turn BFCL, short GSM8K, and MMLU-Pro; lower is better.

Study semantics: each row is one fixed compression method freshly evaluated on the same protocol-v6 validation and sealed splits used by Experiment 1. These reference checks are not agent optimization runs.
